title: Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
subhed: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

Teen stands trial for premeditated murder of Spanish teacher: 'I've ruined my life, it's over'
subhed: Agnès Lassalle was fatally stabbed during class by a student at a high school in Saint-Jean-de-Luz in 2023. The student, who was 16 years old at the time, is standing trial for premeditated murder before a juvenile criminal court from April 21 to 24.

title: How Trump's lingering grudge threatens his appointment of the next Fed chair
subhed: A senator from North Carolina warned that he would not vote in favor of Kevin Warsh, Donald Trump's nominee to succeed Jerome Powell, as long as legal proceedings against the Federal Reserve's chair were still underway.

title: Chinese citizens discover facial recognition fines
subhed: For many people, mass surveillance by the Chinese state primarily targets political dissidents, NGO representatives, or activists of all stripes. However, cameras equipped with advanced technology also sanction the everyday missteps of ordinary citizens.

title: France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
subhed: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.

title: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
subhed: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.

title: Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
subhed: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.
